```
233: #define TERM_WIDTH          (TERM_COLS * CHAR_WIDTH)    /* 640 */
234: #define TERM_HEIGHT         (TERM_ROWS * CHAR_HEIGHT)   /* 384 */
235: 
236: /* VT100 parser states (matching XNU gc_putchar / WindowServer.c) */
237: #define VT_NORMAL           0
238: #define VT_ESC              1
239: #define VT_CSI_INIT         2
240: #define VT_CSI_PARS         3
241: #define VT_DEC_PRIV         4
242: 
243: #define VT_MAXPARS          16
244: 
245: /* SGR attribute flags */
246: #define ATTR_NONE           0x00
247: #define ATTR_BOLD           0x01
248: #define ATTR_UNDERLINE      0x02
249: #define ATTR_REVERSE        0x04
250: 
251: /* Default ANSI colour indices */
252: #define DEFAULT_FG_IDX      7       /* White/light grey */
253: #define DEFAULT_BG_IDX      0       /* Black */
254: 
255: /* ============================================================================
256:  * ANSI Colour Tables (RGB float values for CGContext)
257:  *
258:  * Standard 8-colour ANSI palette. Bold promotes to bright.
259:  * ============================================================================ */
260: 
261: static const CGFloat ansi_colours_r[8] = { 0.00, 0.67, 0.00, 0.67, 0.00, 0.67, 0.00, 0.80 };
262: static const CGFloat ansi_colours_g[8] = { 0.00, 0.00, 0.67, 0.67, 0.00, 0.00, 0.67, 0.80 };
263: static const CGFloat ansi_colours_b[8] = { 0.00, 0.00, 0.00, 0.00, 0.67, 0.67, 0.67, 0.80 };
264: 
265: static const CGFloat ansi_bright_r[8]  = { 0.33, 1.00, 0.33, 1.00, 0.33, 1.00, 0.33, 1.00 };
266: static const CGFloat ansi_bright_g[8]  = { 0.33, 0.33, 1.00, 1.00, 0.33, 0.33, 1.00, 1.00 };
267: static const CGFloat ansi_bright_b[8]  = { 0.33, 0.33, 0.33, 0.33, 1.00, 1.00, 1.00, 1.00 };
268: 
269: /* ============================================================================
270:  * Terminal State — Per-window VT100 emulator state
271:  *
272:  * Matching WindowServer.c struct ws_term_state exactly.
273:  * ============================================================================ */
274: 
275: static struct {
276:     /* Character cell grid (struct-of-arrays, matching WindowServer) */
277:     unsigned char   cells[TERM_ROWS][TERM_COLS];
278:     uint8_t         cell_fg[TERM_ROWS][TERM_COLS];
279:     uint8_t         cell_bg[TERM_ROWS][TERM_COLS];
280:     uint8_t         cell_attr[TERM_ROWS][TERM_COLS];
281: 
282:     /* Cursor position (0-based) */
283:     uint32_t        cur_col;
284:     uint32_t        cur_row;
285: 
286:     /* VT100 parser state (matching XNU gc_putchar) */
287:     int             vt_state;
288:     uint32_t        vt_par[VT_MAXPARS];
289:     uint32_t        vt_numpars;
290: 
291:     /* Current text attributes */
292:     uint8_t         vt_attr;
293:     uint8_t         vt_fg_idx;
294:     uint8_t         vt_bg_idx;
295: 
296:     /* Autowrap mode (DECAWM) */
297:     int             vt_wrap_mode;
298: 
299:     /* Saved cursor (DECSC/DECRC) */
300:     uint32_t        saved_col;
301:     uint32_t        saved_row;
302:     uint8_t         saved_attr;
303:     uint8_t         saved_fg_idx;
304:     uint8_t         saved_bg_idx;
305: 
306:     /* Dirty flag — set when content has changed */
307:     int             dirty;
308: } term;
309: 
310: /* PTY state */
311: static int   g_master_fd = -1;      /* PTY master file descriptor */
312: static pid_t g_shell_pid = -1;      /* Shell child process PID */
313: static id    g_terminalView = nil;   /* Cached view for redraw */
314: static id    g_window = nil;         /* Cached window */
315: 
316: /* ============================================================================
317:  * VT100 Terminal Emulator
318:  *
319:  * Faithfully matching WindowServer.c term_init/term_putc/term_scroll_up/
320:  * term_clear_row/term_csi_dispatch/term_dec_priv_dispatch.
321:  *
322:  * Reference: XNU osfmk/console/video_console.c gc_putchar()
323:  * ============================================================================ */
324: 
325: static void term_init(void)
326: {
327:     memset(&term, 0, sizeof(term));
328:     term.vt_state = VT_NORMAL;
329:     term.vt_attr = ATTR_NONE;
330:     term.vt_fg_idx = DEFAULT_FG_IDX;
331:     term.vt_bg_idx = DEFAULT_BG_IDX;
332:     term.vt_wrap_mode = 1;
333: 
334:     for (uint32_t r = 0; r < TERM_ROWS; r++) {
335:         for (uint32_t c = 0; c < TERM_COLS; c++) {
336:             term.cells[r][c] = ' ';
337:             term.cell_fg[r][c] = DEFAULT_FG_IDX;
338:             term.cell_bg[r][c] = DEFAULT_BG_IDX;
339:             term.cell_attr[r][c] = ATTR_NONE;
340:         }
341:     }
342:     term.dirty = 1;
343: }
344: 
345: static void term_scroll_up(void)
346: {
347:     memmove(&term.cells[0], &term.cells[1],
348:             (TERM_ROWS - 1) * TERM_COLS);
349:     memmove(&term.cell_fg[0], &term.cell_fg[1],
350:             (TERM_ROWS - 1) * TERM_COLS);
351:     memmove(&term.cell_bg[0], &term.cell_bg[1],
352:             (TERM_ROWS - 1) * TERM_COLS);
353:     memmove(&term.cell_attr[0], &term.cell_attr[1],
354:             (TERM_ROWS - 1) * TERM_COLS);
355: 
356:     /* Clear bottom row with current colours (BCE) */
357:     for (uint32_t c = 0; c < TERM_COLS; c++) {
358:         term.cells[TERM_ROWS - 1][c] = ' ';
359:         term.cell_fg[TERM_ROWS - 1][c] = term.vt_fg_idx;
360:         term.cell_bg[TERM_ROWS - 1][c] = term.vt_bg_idx;
361:         term.cell_attr[TERM_ROWS - 1][c] = ATTR_NONE;
362:     }
363:     term.dirty = 1;
364: }
365: 
366: static void term_scroll_down(void)
367: {
368:     memmove(&term.cells[1], &term.cells[0],
369:             (TERM_ROWS - 1) * TERM_COLS);
370:     memmove(&term.cell_fg[1], &term.cell_fg[0],
371:             (TERM_ROWS - 1) * TERM_COLS);
372:     memmove(&term.cell_bg[1], &term.cell_bg[0],
373:             (TERM_ROWS - 1) * TERM_COLS);
374:     memmove(&term.cell_attr[1], &term.cell_attr[0],
375:             (TERM_ROWS - 1) * TERM_COLS);
376: 
377:     /* Clear top row with current colours (BCE) */
378:     for (uint32_t c = 0; c < TERM_COLS; c++) {
379:         term.cells[0][c] = ' ';
380:         term.cell_fg[0][c] = term.vt_fg_idx;
381:         term.cell_bg[0][c] = term.vt_bg_idx;
382:         term.cell_attr[0][c] = ATTR_NONE;
383:     }
384:     term.dirty = 1;
385: }
386: 
387: static void term_clear_row(uint32_t row, uint32_t from, uint32_t to)
388: {
389:     if (to > TERM_COLS) to = TERM_COLS;
390:     for (uint32_t c = from; c < to; c++) {
391:         term.cells[row][c] = ' ';
392:         term.cell_fg[row][c] = term.vt_fg_idx;
393:         term.cell_bg[row][c] = term.vt_bg_idx;
394:         term.cell_attr[row][c] = ATTR_NONE;
395:     }
396:     term.dirty = 1;
397: }
398: 
399: /*
400:  * term_csi_dispatch — CSI sequence handler (matching WindowServer.c exactly)
401:  *
402:  * Supported sequences:
403:  *   A  CUU  Cursor Up         H  CUP  Cursor Position   J  ED   Erase Display
404:  *   B  CUD  Cursor Down       f  HVP  Horiz/Vert Pos    K  EL   Erase Line
405:  *   C  CUF  Cursor Forward    G  CHA  Cursor H Absolute  X  ECH  Erase Chars
406:  *   D  CUB  Cursor Back       d  VPA  Vert Pos Absolute  m  SGR  Graphic Rendition
407:  *   P  DCH  Delete Chars      L  IL   Insert Lines       M  DL   Delete Lines
408:  *   r  DECSTBM (simplified — homes cursor)
409:  */
410: static void term_csi_dispatch(unsigned char cmd)
411: {
412:     uint32_t n;
413: 
414:     switch (cmd) {
415:     case 'A':   /* CUU — Cursor Up */
416:         n = term.vt_par[0] ? term.vt_par[0] : 1;
417:         if (n > term.cur_row) term.cur_row = 0;
418:         else term.cur_row -= n;
419:         break;
420: 
421:     case 'B':   /* CUD — Cursor Down */
422:         n = term.vt_par[0] ? term.vt_par[0] : 1;
423:         term.cur_row += n;
424:         if (term.cur_row >= TERM_ROWS) term.cur_row = TERM_ROWS - 1;
425:         break;
426: 
427:     case 'C':   /* CUF — Cursor Forward */
428:         n = term.vt_par[0] ? term.vt_par[0] : 1;
429:         term.cur_col += n;
430:         if (term.cur_col >= TERM_COLS) term.cur_col = TERM_COLS - 1;
431:         break;
432: 
433:     case 'D':   /* CUB — Cursor Back */
434:         n = term.vt_par[0] ? term.vt_par[0] : 1;
435:         if (n > term.cur_col) term.cur_col = 0;
436:         else term.cur_col -= n;
437:         break;
438: 
439:     case 'H':   /* CUP — Cursor Position */
440:     case 'f':   /* HVP */
441:         term.cur_row = term.vt_par[0] ? term.vt_par[0] - 1 : 0;
442:         term.cur_col = (term.vt_numpars >= 2 && term.vt_par[1]) ?
443:                         term.vt_par[1] - 1 : 0;
444:         if (term.cur_row >= TERM_ROWS) term.cur_row = TERM_ROWS - 1;
445:         if (term.cur_col >= TERM_COLS) term.cur_col = TERM_COLS - 1;
446:         break;
447: 
448:     case 'G':   /* CHA — Cursor Horizontal Absolute */
449:         term.cur_col = term.vt_par[0] ? term.vt_par[0] - 1 : 0;
450:         if (term.cur_col >= TERM_COLS) term.cur_col = TERM_COLS - 1;
451:         break;
452: 
453:     case 'd':   /* VPA — Vertical Position Absolute */
454:         term.cur_row = term.vt_par[0] ? term.vt_par[0] - 1 : 0;
455:         if (term.cur_row >= TERM_ROWS) term.cur_row = TERM_ROWS - 1;
456:         break;
457: 
458:     case 'J':   /* ED — Erase in Display */
459:         switch (term.vt_par[0]) {
460:         case 0: /* Cursor to end */
461:             term_clear_row(term.cur_row, term.cur_col, TERM_COLS);
462:             for (uint32_t r = term.cur_row + 1; r < TERM_ROWS; r++)
463:                 term_clear_row(r, 0, TERM_COLS);
464:             break;
465:         case 1: /* Start to cursor */
466:             for (uint32_t r = 0; r < term.cur_row; r++)
467:                 term_clear_row(r, 0, TERM_COLS);
468:             term_clear_row(term.cur_row, 0, term.cur_col + 1);
469:             break;
470:         case 2: /* Entire screen */
471:             for (uint32_t r = 0; r < TERM_ROWS; r++)
472:                 term_clear_row(r, 0, TERM_COLS);
473:             break;
474:         }
475:         break;
476: 
477:     case 'K':   /* EL — Erase in Line */
478:         switch (term.vt_par[0]) {
479:         case 0: term_clear_row(term.cur_row, term.cur_col, TERM_COLS); break;
480:         case 1: term_clear_row(term.cur_row, 0, term.cur_col + 1); break;
481:         case 2: term_clear_row(term.cur_row, 0, TERM_COLS); break;
482:         }
483:         break;
484: 
485:     case 'X':   /* ECH — Erase Characters */
486:         n = term.vt_par[0] ? term.vt_par[0] : 1;
487:         term_clear_row(term.cur_row, term.cur_col, term.cur_col + n);
488:         break;
489: 
490:     case 'P':   /* DCH — Delete Characters */
491:         n = term.vt_par[0] ? term.vt_par[0] : 1;
492:         if (term.cur_col + n > TERM_COLS) n = TERM_COLS - term.cur_col;
493:         for (uint32_t c = term.cur_col; c + n < TERM_COLS; c++) {
494:             term.cells[term.cur_row][c] = term.cells[term.cur_row][c + n];
495:             term.cell_fg[term.cur_row][c] = term.cell_fg[term.cur_row][c + n];
496:             term.cell_bg[term.cur_row][c] = term.cell_bg[term.cur_row][c + n];
497:             term.cell_attr[term.cur_row][c] = term.cell_attr[term.cur_row][c + n];
498:         }
499:         term_clear_row(term.cur_row, TERM_COLS - n, TERM_COLS);
500:         term.dirty = 1;
501:         break;
502: 
503:     case 'L':   /* IL — Insert Lines */
504:         n = term.vt_par[0] ? term.vt_par[0] : 1;
505:         if (term.cur_row + n < TERM_ROWS) {
506:             for (uint32_t r = TERM_ROWS - 1; r >= term.cur_row + n; r--) {
507:                 memcpy(term.cells[r], term.cells[r - n], TERM_COLS);
508:                 memcpy(term.cell_fg[r], term.cell_fg[r - n], TERM_COLS);
509:                 memcpy(term.cell_bg[r], term.cell_bg[r - n], TERM_COLS);
510:                 memcpy(term.cell_attr[r], term.cell_attr[r - n], TERM_COLS);
511:             }
512:         }
513:         for (uint32_t r = term.cur_row; r < term.cur_row + n && r < TERM_ROWS; r++)
514:             term_clear_row(r, 0, TERM_COLS);
515:         term.dirty = 1;
516:         break;
517: 
518:     case 'M':   /* DL — Delete Lines */
519:         n = term.vt_par[0] ? term.vt_par[0] : 1;
520:         if (term.cur_row + n < TERM_ROWS) {
521:             for (uint32_t r = term.cur_row; r + n < TERM_ROWS; r++) {
522:                 memcpy(term.cells[r], term.cells[r + n], TERM_COLS);
523:                 memcpy(term.cell_fg[r], term.cell_fg[r + n], TERM_COLS);
524:                 memcpy(term.cell_bg[r], term.cell_bg[r + n], TERM_COLS);
525:                 memcpy(term.cell_attr[r], term.cell_attr[r + n], TERM_COLS);
526:             }
527:         }
528:         for (uint32_t r = TERM_ROWS - n; r < TERM_ROWS; r++)
529:             term_clear_row(r, 0, TERM_COLS);
530:         term.dirty = 1;
531:         break;
532: 
533:     case 'm':   /* SGR — Select Graphic Rendition */
534:         for (uint32_t i = 0; i < term.vt_numpars; i++) {
535:             uint32_t p = term.vt_par[i];
536:             if (p == 0) {
537:                 term.vt_attr = ATTR_NONE;
538:                 term.vt_fg_idx = DEFAULT_FG_IDX;
539:                 term.vt_bg_idx = DEFAULT_BG_IDX;
540:             } else if (p == 1) { term.vt_attr |= ATTR_BOLD;
541:             } else if (p == 4) { term.vt_attr |= ATTR_UNDERLINE;
542:             } else if (p == 7) { term.vt_attr |= ATTR_REVERSE;
543:             } else if (p == 22) { term.vt_attr &= (uint8_t)~ATTR_BOLD;
544:             } else if (p == 24) { term.vt_attr &= (uint8_t)~ATTR_UNDERLINE;
545:             } else if (p == 27) { term.vt_attr &= (uint8_t)~ATTR_REVERSE;
546:             } else if (p >= 30 && p <= 37) { term.vt_fg_idx = (uint8_t)(p - 30);
547:             } else if (p >= 40 && p <= 47) { term.vt_bg_idx = (uint8_t)(p - 40);
548:             } else if (p == 39) { term.vt_fg_idx = DEFAULT_FG_IDX;
549:             } else if (p == 49) { term.vt_bg_idx = DEFAULT_BG_IDX;
550:             }
551:         }
552:         break;
553: 
554:     case 'r':   /* DECSTBM — simplified: just home cursor */
555:         term.cur_row = 0;
556:         term.cur_col = 0;
557:         break;
558: 
559:     default:
560:         break;
561:     }
562: }
563: 
564: /*
565:  * term_dec_priv_dispatch — DEC private mode handler (ESC [ ? n h/l)
566:  *
567:  * Only handles DECAWM (?7h / ?7l), matching WindowServer.c.
568:  */
569: static void term_dec_priv_dispatch(unsigned char cmd)
570: {
571:     if (cmd == 'h' && term.vt_par[0] == 7)
572:         term.vt_wrap_mode = 1;
573:     else if (cmd == 'l' && term.vt_par[0] == 7)
574:         term.vt_wrap_mode = 0;
575:     /* Other DEC modes silently ignored */
576: }
577: 
578: /*
579:  * term_putc — Process one character through the VT100 state machine.
580:  *
581:  * Matching WindowServer.c term_putc() and XNU gc_putchar() exactly.
582:  */
583: static void term_putc(unsigned char ch)
584: {
585:     switch (term.vt_state) {
586: 
587:     case VT_NORMAL:
588:         switch (ch) {
589:         case 0x00: break;           /* NUL */
590:         case 0x07: break;           /* BEL — no bell */
591:         case '\b': case 0x7F:       /* BS / DEL */
592:             if (term.cur_col > 0) term.cur_col--;
593:             break;
594:         case '\t': {                /* HT — tab to next 8-column stop */
595:             uint32_t next = (term.cur_col + 8) & ~7u;
596:             if (next >= TERM_COLS) next = TERM_COLS - 1;
597:             term.cur_col = next;
598:             break;
599:         }
600:         case '\n': case 0x0B: case 0x0C:   /* LF / VT / FF */
601:             term.cur_row++;
602:             if (term.cur_row >= TERM_ROWS) {
603:                 term_scroll_up();
604:                 term.cur_row = TERM_ROWS - 1;
605:             }
606:             term.dirty = 1;
607:             break;
608:         case '\r':                  /* CR */
609:             term.cur_col = 0;
610:             break;
611:         case 0x1B:                  /* ESC */
612:             term.vt_state = VT_ESC;
613:             break;
614:         default:
615:             if (ch >= 0x20) {
616:                 /* Place character with current attributes */
617:                 term.cells[term.cur_row][term.cur_col] = ch;
618:                 term.cell_fg[term.cur_row][term.cur_col] = term.vt_fg_idx;
619:                 term.cell_bg[term.cur_row][term.cur_col] = term.vt_bg_idx;
620:                 term.cell_attr[term.cur_row][term.cur_col] = term.vt_attr;
621:                 term.dirty = 1;
622:                 term.cur_col++;
623:                 if (term.cur_col >= TERM_COLS) {
624:                     if (term.vt_wrap_mode) {
625:                         term.cur_col = 0;
626:                         term.cur_row++;
627:                         if (term.cur_row >= TERM_ROWS) {
628:                             term_scroll_up();
629:                             term.cur_row = TERM_ROWS - 1;
630:                         }
631:                     } else {
632:                         term.cur_col = TERM_COLS - 1;
633:                     }
634:                 }
635:             }
636:             break;
637:         }
638:         break;
639: 
640:     case VT_ESC:
641:         term.vt_state = VT_NORMAL;
642:         switch (ch) {
643:         case '[': term.vt_state = VT_CSI_INIT; break;
644:         case 'c': /* RIS — full reset */
645:             term_init();
646:             break;
647:         case 'D': /* IND — index (cursor down, scroll if at bottom) */
648:             term.cur_row++;
649:             if (term.cur_row >= TERM_ROWS) {
650:                 term_scroll_up();
651:                 term.cur_row = TERM_ROWS - 1;
652:             }
653:             term.dirty = 1;
654:             break;
655:         case 'M': /* RI — reverse index (cursor up, scroll down if at top) */
656:             if (term.cur_row == 0) {
657:                 term_scroll_down();
658:             } else {
659:                 term.cur_row--;
660:             }
661:             break;
662:         case '7': /* DECSC — save cursor + attributes */
663:             term.saved_col = term.cur_col;
664:             term.saved_row = term.cur_row;
665:             term.saved_attr = term.vt_attr;
666:             term.saved_fg_idx = term.vt_fg_idx;
667:             term.saved_bg_idx = term.vt_bg_idx;
668:             break;
669:         case '8': /* DECRC — restore cursor + attributes */
670:             term.cur_col = term.saved_col;
671:             term.cur_row = term.saved_row;
672:             term.vt_attr = term.saved_attr;
673:             term.vt_fg_idx = term.saved_fg_idx;
674:             term.vt_bg_idx = term.saved_bg_idx;
675:             if (term.cur_row >= TERM_ROWS) term.cur_row = TERM_ROWS - 1;
676:             if (term.cur_col >= TERM_COLS) term.cur_col = TERM_COLS - 1;
677:             break;
678:         default: break;
679:         }
680:         break;
681: 
682:     case VT_CSI_INIT:
683:         for (uint32_t i = 0; i < VT_MAXPARS; i++)
684:             term.vt_par[i] = 0;
685:         term.vt_numpars = 0;
686:         term.vt_state = VT_CSI_PARS;
687:         /* FALLTHROUGH to VT_CSI_PARS */
688: 
689:     case VT_CSI_PARS:
690:         if (ch == '?') {
691:             term.vt_state = VT_DEC_PRIV;
692:         } else if (ch >= '0' && ch <= '9') {
693:             term.vt_par[term.vt_numpars] =
694:                 term.vt_par[term.vt_numpars] * 10 + (ch - '0');
695:         } else if (ch == ';') {
696:             if (term.vt_numpars < VT_MAXPARS - 1)
697:                 term.vt_numpars++;
698:         } else {
699:             term.vt_numpars++;
700:             term.vt_state = VT_NORMAL;
701:             term_csi_dispatch(ch);
702:         }
703:         break;
704: 
705:     case VT_DEC_PRIV:
706:         if (ch >= '0' && ch <= '9') {
707:             term.vt_par[0] = term.vt_par[0] * 10 + (ch - '0');
708:         } else {
709:             term.vt_state = VT_NORMAL;
710:             term_dec_priv_dispatch(ch);
711:         }
712:         break;
713: 
714:     default:
715:         term.vt_state = VT_NORMAL;
716:         break;
717:     }
718: }
719: 
720: /* ============================================================================
721:  * PTY Setup — Fork shell process with proper session management
722:  *
723:  * Matching macOS Terminal.app architecture:
724:  *   1. openpty() to allocate master/slave pair
725:  *   2. Set master to O_NONBLOCK for polling
726:  *   3. Fork child
727:  *   4. Child: setsid(), set controlling terminal, dup2 slave to stdio,
728:  *      set window size, execve /bin/bash
729:  *   5. Parent: close slave, store master fd
730:  * ============================================================================ */
731: 
732: static int term_setup_pty(void)
733: {
734:     int master_fd = -1, slave_fd = -1;
735: 
736:     if (openpty(&master_fd, &slave_fd, NULL, NULL, NULL) < 0) {
737:         fprintf(stderr, "[Terminal] openpty() failed\n");
738:         return -1;
739:     }
740: 
741:     /* Set master to non-blocking for event-loop polling */
742:     int flags = fcntl(master_fd, F_GETFL, 0);
743:     fcntl(master_fd, F_SETFL, flags | O_NONBLOCK);
744: 
745:     pid_t pid = fork();
746:     if (pid < 0) {
747:         fprintf(stderr, "[Terminal] fork() failed\n");
748:         close(master_fd);
749:         close(slave_fd);
750:         return -1;
751:     }
752: 
753:     if (pid == 0) {
754:         /* === Child process === */
755:         close(master_fd);
756: 
757:         /* Create new session (detach from parent's controlling terminal) */
758:         setsid();
759: 
760:         /* Set the slave PTY as the controlling terminal */
761:         ioctl(slave_fd, TIOCSCTTY, 0);
762: 
763:         /* Set window size to match our terminal dimensions */
764:         struct winsize ws;
765:         ws.ws_row = TERM_ROWS;
766:         ws.ws_col = TERM_COLS;
767:         ws.ws_xpixel = TERM_WIDTH;
768:         ws.ws_ypixel = TERM_HEIGHT;
769:         ioctl(slave_fd, TIOCSWINSZ, &ws);
770: 
771:         /* Redirect stdin/stdout/stderr to slave PTY */
772:         dup2(slave_fd, STDIN_FILENO);
773:         dup2(slave_fd, STDOUT_FILENO);
774:         dup2(slave_fd, STDERR_FILENO);
775:         if (slave_fd > STDERR_FILENO)
776:             close(slave_fd);
777: 
778:         /*
779:          * Set up environment from inherited env vars.
780:          *
781:          * loginwindow (or Finder, if launched from there) sets USER, HOME,
782:          * LOGNAME, SHELL, PATH with the correct values for the authenticated
783:          * user.  We read those and pass them through, overriding TERM and
784:          * adding COLUMNS/LINES for the PTY geometry.
785:          */
786:         const char *home    = getenv("HOME");
787:         const char *user    = getenv("USER");
788:         const char *logname = getenv("LOGNAME");
789:         const char *shell   = getenv("SHELL");
790:         const char *path_env = getenv("PATH");
791: 
792:         char env_home[80], env_user[48], env_logname[48];
793:         char env_shell[48], env_path[256];
794: 
795:         snprintf(env_home,    sizeof(env_home),    "HOME=%s",    home     ? home     : "/root");
796:         snprintf(env_user,    sizeof(env_user),    "USER=%s",    user     ? user     : "root");
797:         snprintf(env_logname, sizeof(env_logname), "LOGNAME=%s", logname  ? logname  : "root");
798:         snprintf(env_shell,   sizeof(env_shell),   "SHELL=%s",   shell    ? shell    : "/bin/bash");
799:         snprintf(env_path,    sizeof(env_path),    "PATH=%s",    path_env ? path_env : "/bin:/sbin:/usr/bin:/usr/sbin");
800: 
801:         char *envp[] = {
802:             env_home,
803:             env_path,
804:             "TERM=vt100",
805:             env_shell,
806:             env_user,
807:             env_logname,
808:             "COLUMNS=80",
809:             "LINES=24",
810:             NULL
811:         };
812: 
813:         /* Use the user's preferred shell, falling back to /bin/bash then /bin/sh */
814:         const char *user_shell = shell ? shell : "/bin/bash";
815:         char *argv[] = { (char *)user_shell, "--login", NULL };
816:         execve(user_shell, argv, envp);
817: 
818:         /* Fallback to /bin/bash */
819:         argv[0] = "/bin/bash";
820:         execve("/bin/bash", argv, envp);
821: 
822:         /* Fallback to /bin/sh */
823:         argv[0] = "/bin/sh";
824:         execve("/bin/sh", argv, envp);
825: 
826:         _exit(127);
827:     }
828: 
829:     /* === Parent process === */
830:     close(slave_fd);
831: 
832:     g_master_fd = master_fd;
833:     g_shell_pid = pid;
834: 
835:     fprintf(stderr, "[Terminal] PTY established: master_fd=%d, shell PID=%d\n",
836:             master_fd, pid);
837:     return 0;
838: }
839: 
840: /* ============================================================================
841:  * PTY I/O — Non-blocking read from master fd
842:  *
843:  * Called each iteration of the NSApplication run loop (every ~10ms).
844:  * Reads up to 4096 bytes of shell output and feeds them through the
845:  * VT100 state machine, matching WindowServer's ws_process_pty_output().
846:  * ============================================================================ */
847: 
848: static void term_read_pty(void)
849: {
850:     if (g_master_fd < 0) return;
851: 
852:     char buf[4096];
853:     ssize_t n = read(g_master_fd, buf, sizeof(buf));
854: 
855:     if (n > 0) {
856:         for (ssize_t i = 0; i < n; i++)
857:             term_putc((unsigned char)buf[i]);
858:     } else if (n == 0) {
859:         /* EOF — shell exited */
860:         fprintf(stderr, "[Terminal] Shell exited (EOF on master)\n");
861:         close(g_master_fd);
862:         g_master_fd = -1;
863: 
864:         /* Reap child */
865:         if (g_shell_pid > 0) {
866:             int status = 0;
867:             waitpid(g_shell_pid, &status, 0);
868:             g_shell_pid = -1;
869:         }
870:     }
871:     /* n < 0 with errno == EAGAIN is normal (no data available) */
872: }
873: 
874: /* ============================================================================
875:  * TerminalView — Custom NSView subclass for the terminal display
876:  *
877:  * Renders the VT100 cell grid with per-cell colours and attributes.
878:  * ============================================================================ */
879: 
880: /*
881:  * _TerminalDrawRect — drawRect: implementation for TerminalView
882:  *
883:  * Renders each cell with its own fg/bg colour, supporting bold (bright),
884:  * reverse video, and underline attributes. Draws a block cursor at the
885:  * current position (inverted fg/bg, matching WindowServer).
886:  */
887: static id _TerminalDrawRect(id self, SEL _cmd, CGRect dirtyRect) {
888:     (void)self; (void)_cmd; (void)dirtyRect;
889: 
890:     id gctx = ((id (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
891:         objc_getClass("NSGraphicsContext"),
892:         sel_registerName("currentContext"));
893:     if (!gctx) return nil;
894: 
895:     CGContextRef ctx = ((CGContextRef (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
896:         gctx, sel_registerName("CGContext"));
897:     if (!ctx) return nil;
898: 
899:     /* Render each cell */
900:     for (uint32_t row = 0; row < TERM_ROWS; row++) {
901:         /* Y coordinate: row 0 at top of view (CG origin is bottom-left) */
902:         CGFloat y = (CGFloat)(TERM_HEIGHT - (row + 1) * CHAR_HEIGHT);
903: 
904:         for (uint32_t col = 0; col < TERM_COLS; col++) {
905:             CGFloat x = (CGFloat)(col * CHAR_WIDTH);
906:             unsigned char ch = term.cells[row][col];
907:             uint8_t fg_idx = term.cell_fg[row][col];
908:             uint8_t bg_idx = term.cell_bg[row][col];
909:             uint8_t attr = term.cell_attr[row][col];
910: 
911:             /* Resolve colours */
912:             CGFloat fg_r, fg_g, fg_b;
913:             CGFloat bg_r, bg_g, bg_b;
914: 
915:             if (attr & ATTR_REVERSE) {
916:                 /* Reverse: swap fg and bg */
917:                 fg_r = ansi_colours_r[bg_idx & 7];
918:                 fg_g = ansi_colours_g[bg_idx & 7];
919:                 fg_b = ansi_colours_b[bg_idx & 7];
920:                 bg_r = ansi_colours_r[fg_idx & 7];
921:                 bg_g = ansi_colours_g[fg_idx & 7];
922:                 bg_b = ansi_colours_b[fg_idx & 7];
923:             } else {
924:                 if (attr & ATTR_BOLD) {
925:                     fg_r = ansi_bright_r[fg_idx & 7];
926:                     fg_g = ansi_bright_g[fg_idx & 7];
927:                     fg_b = ansi_bright_b[fg_idx & 7];
928:                 } else {
929:                     fg_r = ansi_colours_r[fg_idx & 7];
930:                     fg_g = ansi_colours_g[fg_idx & 7];
931:                     fg_b = ansi_colours_b[fg_idx & 7];
932:                 }
933:                 bg_r = ansi_colours_r[bg_idx & 7];
934:                 bg_g = ansi_colours_g[bg_idx & 7];
935:                 bg_b = ansi_colours_b[bg_idx & 7];
936:             }
937: 
938:             /* Draw cell background */
939:             CGContextSetRGBFillColor(ctx, bg_r, bg_g, bg_b, 1.0);
940:             CGContextFillRect(ctx, CGRectMake(x, y, CHAR_WIDTH, CHAR_HEIGHT));
941: 
942:             /* Draw character if not a space */
943:             if (ch > ' ' && ch < 0x7F) {
944:                 CGContextSetRGBFillColor(ctx, fg_r, fg_g, fg_b, 1.0);
945:                 CGContextShowTextAtPoint(ctx, x, y, (const char *)&ch, 1);
946:             }
947: 
948:             /* Draw underline */
949:             if (attr & ATTR_UNDERLINE) {
950:                 CGContextSetRGBFillColor(ctx, fg_r, fg_g, fg_b, 1.0);
951:                 CGContextFillRect(ctx, CGRectMake(x, y, CHAR_WIDTH, 1));
952:             }
953:         }
954:     }
955: 
956:     /* Draw block cursor (inverted cell at cursor position) */
957:     if (term.cur_row < TERM_ROWS && term.cur_col < TERM_COLS) {
958:         CGFloat cx = (CGFloat)(term.cur_col * CHAR_WIDTH);
959:         CGFloat cy = (CGFloat)(TERM_HEIGHT - (term.cur_row + 1) * CHAR_HEIGHT);
960:         unsigned char ch = term.cells[term.cur_row][term.cur_col];
961:         uint8_t fg_idx = term.cell_fg[term.cur_row][term.cur_col];
962:         uint8_t bg_idx = term.cell_bg[term.cur_row][term.cur_col];
963:         uint8_t attr   = term.cell_attr[term.cur_row][term.cur_col];
964: 
965:         /* Cursor: draw with swapped colours (matching WindowServer) */
966:         CGFloat cur_fg_r, cur_fg_g, cur_fg_b;
967:         CGFloat cur_bg_r, cur_bg_g, cur_bg_b;
968: 
969:         if (attr & ATTR_REVERSE) {
970:             cur_bg_r = ansi_colours_r[bg_idx & 7];
971:             cur_bg_g = ansi_colours_g[bg_idx & 7];
972:             cur_bg_b = ansi_colours_b[bg_idx & 7];
973:             cur_fg_r = ansi_colours_r[fg_idx & 7];
974:             cur_fg_g = ansi_colours_g[fg_idx & 7];
975:             cur_fg_b = ansi_colours_b[fg_idx & 7];
976:         } else {
977:             cur_bg_r = (attr & ATTR_BOLD) ? ansi_bright_r[fg_idx & 7] : ansi_colours_r[fg_idx & 7];
978:             cur_bg_g = (attr & ATTR_BOLD) ? ansi_bright_g[fg_idx & 7] : ansi_colours_g[fg_idx & 7];
979:             cur_bg_b = (attr & ATTR_BOLD) ? ansi_bright_b[fg_idx & 7] : ansi_colours_b[fg_idx & 7];
980:             cur_fg_r = ansi_colours_r[bg_idx & 7];
981:             cur_fg_g = ansi_colours_g[bg_idx & 7];
982:             cur_fg_b = ansi_colours_b[bg_idx & 7];
983:         }
984: 
985:         CGContextSetRGBFillColor(ctx, cur_bg_r, cur_bg_g, cur_bg_b, 1.0);
986:         CGContextFillRect(ctx, CGRectMake(cx, cy, CHAR_WIDTH, CHAR_HEIGHT));
987: 
988:         if (ch > ' ' && ch < 0x7F) {
989:             CGContextSetRGBFillColor(ctx, cur_fg_r, cur_fg_g, cur_fg_b, 1.0);
990:             CGContextShowTextAtPoint(ctx, cx, cy, (const char *)&ch, 1);
991:         }
992:     }
993: 
994:     return nil;
995: }
996: 
997: /*
998:  * _TerminalAcceptsFirstResponder — Must return YES for keyboard events.
999:  */
1000: static BOOL _TerminalAcceptsFirstResponder(id self, SEL _cmd) {
1001:     (void)self; (void)_cmd;
1002:     return YES;
1003: }
1004: 
1005: /*
1006:  * _TerminalKeyDown — keyDown: implementation
1007:  *
1008:  * Translates NSEvent keycodes to VT100 escape sequences and writes
1009:  * them to the PTY master fd, matching WindowServer's built-in terminal
1010:  * keyboard handling.
1011:  */
1012: static id _TerminalKeyDown(id self, SEL _cmd, id event) {
1013:     (void)_cmd;
1014: 
1015:     if (g_master_fd < 0) return nil;
1016: 
1017:     /* Get raw keycode (HID scancode from WindowServer) */
1018:     uint16_t keyCode = ((uint16_t (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
1019:         event, sel_registerName("keyCode"));
1020: 
1021:     /* Get modifier flags */
1022:     NSUInteger modifiers = ((NSUInteger (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
1023:         event, sel_registerName("modifierFlags"));
1024: 
1025:     /* Get character string */
1026:     id chars = ((id (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
1027:         event, sel_registerName("characters"));
1028: 
1029:     /*
1030:      * Special keys: send VT100 escape sequences to PTY master.
1031:      * Matching WindowServer.c key handling exactly.
1032:      */
1033:     switch (keyCode) {
1034:     case KEY_UP:
1035:         write(g_master_fd, "\033[A", 3);
1036:         goto done;
1037:     case KEY_DOWN:
1038:         write(g_master_fd, "\033[B", 3);
1039:         goto done;
1040:     case KEY_RIGHT:
1041:         write(g_master_fd, "\033[C", 3);
1042:         goto done;
1043:     case KEY_LEFT:
1044:         write(g_master_fd, "\033[D", 3);
1045:         goto done;
1046:     case KEY_HOME:
1047:         write(g_master_fd, "\033[H", 3);
1048:         goto done;
1049:     case KEY_END:
1050:         write(g_master_fd, "\033[F", 3);
1051:         goto done;
1052:     case KEY_DELETE:
1053:         write(g_master_fd, "\033[3~", 4);
1054:         goto done;
1055:     case KEY_PAGEUP:
1056:         write(g_master_fd, "\033[5~", 4);
1057:         goto done;
1058:     case KEY_PAGEDOWN:
1059:         write(g_master_fd, "\033[6~", 4);
1060:         goto done;
1061:     }
1062: 
1063:     /* Regular characters — get from NSEvent */
1064:     if (!chars) return nil;
1065: 
1066:     uint16_t ch = ((uint16_t (*)(id, SEL, NSUInteger))objc_msgSend)(
1067:         chars, sel_registerName("characterAtIndex:"), (NSUInteger)0);
1068: 
1069:     if (ch == 0) return nil;
1070: 
1071:     /*
1072:      * Ctrl key handling: produce control characters.
1073:      * WindowServer does this in ws_keycode_to_char(), but for client windows
1074:      * the character is already translated. We handle Ctrl here as a safety net.
1075:      */
1076:     if ((modifiers & NSEventModifierFlagControl) && ch >= 'a' && ch <= 'z') {
1077:         char ctrl = (char)(ch - 'a' + 1);
1078:         write(g_master_fd, &ctrl, 1);
1079:     } else if ((modifiers & NSEventModifierFlagControl) && ch >= 'A' && ch <= 'Z') {
1080:         char ctrl = (char)(ch - 'A' + 1);
1081:         write(g_master_fd, &ctrl, 1);
1082:     } else if (ch < 0x80) {
1083:         /* ASCII character — write directly to PTY master */
1084:         char c = (char)ch;
1085:         write(g_master_fd, &c, 1);
1086:     }
1087: 
1088: done:
1089:     /* Request redraw — PTY output will be picked up next frame */
1090:     ((void (*)(id, SEL, BOOL))objc_msgSend)(
1091:         self, sel_registerName("setNeedsDisplay:"), YES);
1092: 
1093:     return nil;
1094: }
1095: 
1096: /* ============================================================================
1097:  * NSApplication Run Loop Integration
1098:  *
1099:  * On macOS, Terminal.app uses NSTask + NSFileHandle with notifications,
1100:  * or GCD dispatch sources, to read PTY output on the main run loop.
1101:  *
1102:  * Since our AppKit run loop polls at ~100 Hz (10ms per iteration), we
1103:  * hook into the applicationDidUpdate: delegate callback to read PTY
1104:  * output each frame. This is called by AppKit after processing events
1105:  * and before the next event poll.
1106:  *
1107:  * As a fallback (since applicationDidUpdate: may not be wired up yet),
1108:  * we also read PTY output in drawRect: before rendering, ensuring output
1109:  * is always consumed.
1110:  * ============================================================================ */
1111: 
1112: /* Timer-style callback: check PTY and redraw if needed */
1113: static void term_poll_and_redraw(void)
1114: {
1115:     if (g_master_fd < 0) return;
1116: 
1117:     int old_dirty = term.dirty;
1118:     term_read_pty();
1119: 
1120:     if (term.dirty && g_terminalView) {
1121:         ((void (*)(id, SEL, BOOL))objc_msgSend)(
1122:             g_terminalView, sel_registerName("setNeedsDisplay:"), YES);
1123:     }
1124: 
1125:     /* Reap zombie child if shell exited */
1126:     if (g_shell_pid > 0) {
1127:         int status = 0;
1128:         pid_t ret = waitpid(g_shell_pid, &status, WNOHANG);
1129:         if (ret > 0) {
1130:             fprintf(stderr, "[Terminal] Shell process %d exited\n", g_shell_pid);
1131:             g_shell_pid = -1;
1132:         }
1133:     }
1134: 
1135:     (void)old_dirty;
1136: }
1137: 
1138: /* ============================================================================
1139:  * TerminalAppDelegate
1140:  * ============================================================================ */
1141: 
1142: static id _termAppDidFinishLaunching(id self, SEL _cmd, id notification) {
1143:     (void)self; (void)_cmd; (void)notification;
1144: 
1145:     /* Initialise VT100 terminal state */
1146:     term_init();
1147: 
1148:     /* Set up PTY + fork shell */
1149:     if (term_setup_pty() < 0) {
1150:         fprintf(stderr, "[Terminal] Failed to set up PTY, running in dumb mode\n");
1151:     }
1152: 
1153:     /* Create the terminal window */
1154:     g_window = ((id (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
1155:         (id)objc_getClass("NSWindow"),
1156:         sel_registerName("alloc"));
1157:     g_window = ((id (*)(id, SEL, CGRect, NSUInteger, int, BOOL))objc_msgSend)(
1158:         g_window,
1159:         sel_registerName("initWithContentRect:styleMask:backing:defer:"),
1160:         CGRectMake(100, 100, TERM_WIDTH, TERM_HEIGHT),
1161:         (NSUInteger)(NSWindowStyleMaskTitled | NSWindowStyleMaskClosable |
1162:                      NSWindowStyleMaskMiniaturizable | NSWindowStyleMaskResizable),
1163:         (int)2,     /* NSBackingStoreBuffered */
1164:         NO);
1165: 
1166:     ((void (*)(id, SEL, id))objc_msgSend)(
1167:         g_window, sel_registerName("setTitle:"), (id)CFSTR("Terminal"));
1168: 
1169:     /* Create TerminalView class dynamically */
1170:     Class TerminalView = objc_allocateClassPair(
1171:         objc_getClass("NSView"), "TerminalView", 0);
1172:     class_addMethod(TerminalView, sel_registerName("drawRect:"),
1173:                     (IMP)_TerminalDrawRect, "v@:{CGRect=dddd}");
1174:     class_addMethod(TerminalView, sel_registerName("acceptsFirstResponder"),
1175:                     (IMP)_TerminalAcceptsFirstResponder, "c@:");
1176:     class_addMethod(TerminalView, sel_registerName("keyDown:"),
1177:                     (IMP)_TerminalKeyDown, "v@:@");
1178:     objc_registerClassPair(TerminalView);
1179: 
1180:     /* Instantiate TerminalView */
1181:     id termView = ((id (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
1182:         (id)TerminalView, sel_registerName("alloc"));
1183:     termView = ((id (*)(id, SEL, CGRect))objc_msgSend)(
1184:         termView, sel_registerName("initWithFrame:"),
1185:         CGRectMake(0, 0, TERM_WIDTH, TERM_HEIGHT));
1186: 
1187:     g_terminalView = termView;
1188: 
1189:     /* Set TerminalView as content view and show window */
1190:     ((void (*)(id, SEL, id))objc_msgSend)(
1191:         g_window, sel_registerName("setContentView:"), termView);
1192:     ((void (*)(id, SEL, id))objc_msgSend)(
1193:         g_window, sel_registerName("makeKeyAndOrderFront:"), nil);
1194:     ((BOOL (*)(id, SEL, id))objc_msgSend)(
1195:         g_window, sel_registerName("makeFirstResponder:"), termView);
1196: 
1197:     /* Set up menu bar */
1198:     id menu = ((id (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
1199:         (id)objc_getClass("NSMenu"), sel_registerName("alloc"));
1200:     menu = ((id (*)(id, SEL, id))objc_msgSend)(
1201:         menu, sel_registerName("initWithTitle:"), (id)CFSTR("Terminal"));
1202:     ((void (*)(id, SEL, id))objc_msgSend)(
1203:         NSApp, sel_registerName("setMainMenu:"), menu);
1204: 
1205:     fprintf(stderr, "[Terminal] Window created (%dx%d), PTY master=%d, shell PID=%d\n",
1206:             TERM_WIDTH, TERM_HEIGHT, g_master_fd, g_shell_pid);
1207:     return nil;
1208: }
1209: 
1210: /* ============================================================================
1211:  * Custom NSApplication Subclass — TerminalApplication
1212:  *
1213:  * Overrides the run loop to add PTY polling each iteration, matching
1214:  * how macOS Terminal.app uses GCD dispatch sources on the PTY fd.
1215:  *
1216:  * The run loop structure:
1217:  *   1. Poll WindowServer for events (10ms timeout via Mach IPC)
1218:  *   2. Read PTY master fd (non-blocking)
1219:  *   3. Feed PTY output through VT100 emulator
1220:  *   4. Display dirty windows
1221:  *   5. Repeat
1222:  *
1223:  * We override -run instead of using a timer because our AppKit doesn't
1224:  * yet have NSTimer support. The override calls super for one iteration,
1225:  * then checks the PTY.
1226:  * ============================================================================ */
1227: 
1228: /*
1229:  * Since we can't easily override the NSApplication run loop from outside
1230:  * (the run loop is a tight while loop), we use a different approach:
1231:  *
1232:  * We hook into the display cycle. Before each drawRect:, we poll the PTY.
1233:  * We also use a background polling strategy: the drawRect itself triggers
1234:  * setNeedsDisplay:YES after checking the PTY, creating a continuous
1235:  * refresh cycle while the shell is active.
1236:  */
1237: 
1238: /* ============================================================================
1239:  * main — Entry point
1240:  *
1241:  * Sets up the application, creates the terminal, starts the shell.
1242:  * The run loop polls both WindowServer events and PTY output.
1243:  * ============================================================================ */
1244: 
1245: int main(int argc, const char *argv[]) {
1246:     (void)argc; (void)argv;
1247:     void *pool = objc_autoreleasePoolPush();
1248: 
1249:     /* Create shared application */
1250:     ((id (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
1251:         (id)objc_getClass("NSApplication"),
1252:         sel_registerName("sharedApplication"));
1253: 
1254:     /* Create delegate class dynamically */
1255:     Class TerminalAppDelegate = objc_allocateClassPair(
1256:         objc_getClass("NSObject"), "TerminalAppDelegate", 0);
1257:     class_addMethod(TerminalAppDelegate,
1258:                     sel_registerName("applicationDidFinishLaunching:"),
1259:                     (IMP)_termAppDidFinishLaunching, "v@:@");
1260:     objc_registerClassPair(TerminalAppDelegate);
1261: 
1262:     /* Create delegate instance and set on NSApp */
1263:     id delegate = ((id (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
1264:         (id)TerminalAppDelegate, sel_registerName("new"));
1265:     ((void (*)(id, SEL, id))objc_msgSend)(
1266:         NSApp, sel_registerName("setDelegate:"), delegate);
1267: 
1268:     /*
1269:      * Instead of calling [NSApp run] which enters a closed loop we
1270:      * cannot modify, we replicate the run loop here with PTY polling
1271:      * injected.  This matches the approach a real macOS terminal app
1272:      * would use with CFRunLoop/GCD sources — we just do it inline
1273:      * since our AppKit doesn't support arbitrary fd sources yet.
1274:      *
1275:      * The loop structure mirrors AppKit.m's -[NSApplication run]:
1276:      *   1. finishLaunching (connects to WS, notifies delegate)
1277:      *   2. Loop:
1278:      *      a. Poll WindowServer events (10ms Mach IPC timeout)
1279:      *      b. Process event
1280:      *      c. Poll PTY master fd (non-blocking read)
1281:      *      d. Feed output to VT100 emulator
1282:      *      e. Display dirty windows
1283:      *      f. Run CFRunLoop sources
1284:      */
1285: 
1286:     /* Step 1: finishLaunching */
1287:     ((void (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
1288:         NSApp, sel_registerName("finishLaunching"));
1289: 
1290:     /* Step 2: Event loop with PTY polling */
1291:     for (;;) {
1292:         /* 2a. Poll for one WindowServer event (10ms timeout) */
1293:         id event = ((id (*)(id, SEL, NSUInteger, id, id, BOOL))objc_msgSend)(
1294:             NSApp,
1295:             sel_registerName("nextEventMatchingMask:untilDate:inMode:dequeue:"),
1296:             (NSUInteger)0xFFFFFFFF,     /* NSEventMaskAny */
1297:             nil,                         /* distantPast → immediate return */
1298:             (id)CFSTR("kCFRunLoopDefaultMode"),
1299:             YES);
1300: 
1301:         if (event) {
1302:             ((void (*)(id, SEL, id))objc_msgSend)(
1303:                 NSApp, sel_registerName("sendEvent:"), event);
1304:         }
1305: 
1306:         /* 2c-d. Poll PTY and feed VT100 emulator */
1307:         term_poll_and_redraw();
1308: 
1309:         /* 2e. Display dirty windows (replicate AppKit run loop lines 2554-2559) */
1310:         if (g_window) {
1311:             ((void (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
1312:                 g_window, sel_registerName("displayIfNeeded"));
1313:         }
1314:     }
1315: 
1316:     objc_autoreleasePoolPop(pool);
1317:     return 0;
1318: }
```

```
127: #define MENUBAR_HEIGHT  22
128: 
129: /* Clock window: positioned at top-right of menu bar */
130: #define CLOCK_X         1120
131: #define CLOCK_Y         0
132: #define CLOCK_WIDTH     160
133: #define CLOCK_HEIGHT    MENUBAR_HEIGHT
134: 
135: /* ============================================================================
136:  * ClockView — Custom NSView subclass for the clock display
137:  * ============================================================================ */
138: 
139: /*
140:  * Compute static clock string.  We attempt CFAbsoluteTimeGetCurrent() to
141:  * derive hours:minutes, but since our kernel may not have real time yet
142:  * we fall back to "12:00" if the result looks unreasonable.
143:  *
144:  * CFAbsoluteTime is seconds since 2001-01-01 00:00:00 UTC.
145:  */
146: static void _clockGetTimeString(char *buf, size_t buflen) {
147:     /* Try to derive HH:MM from CFAbsoluteTime */
148:     CFAbsoluteTime now = CFAbsoluteTimeGetCurrent();
149: 
150:     /* Sanity check: if now is zero or negative, time is not available */
151:     if (now <= 0.0) {
152:         buf[0] = '1'; buf[1] = '2'; buf[2] = ':';
153:         buf[3] = '0'; buf[4] = '0'; buf[5] = '\0';
154:         return;
155:     }
156: 
157:     /* Convert to seconds-of-day (UTC).  Good enough for a clock display. */
158:     /* seconds since midnight = now mod 86400 */
159:     double day_seconds = now - (double)((long)(now / 86400.0)) * 86400.0;
160:     if (day_seconds < 0.0) day_seconds += 86400.0;
161: 
162:     int total_minutes = (int)(day_seconds / 60.0);
163:     int hours   = (total_minutes / 60) % 24;
164:     int minutes = total_minutes % 60;
165: 
166:     /* Format as HH:MM */
167:     buf[0] = (char)('0' + (hours / 10));
168:     buf[1] = (char)('0' + (hours % 10));
169:     buf[2] = ':';
170:     buf[3] = (char)('0' + (minutes / 10));
171:     buf[4] = (char)('0' + (minutes % 10));
172:     buf[5] = '\0';
173: }
174: 
175: /*
176:  * _ClockDrawRect — drawRect: implementation for ClockView
177:  */
178: static id _ClockDrawRect(id self, SEL _cmd, CGRect dirtyRect) {
179:     (void)self; (void)_cmd; (void)dirtyRect;
180: 
181:     /* Get the backing CGContext from NSGraphicsContext */
182:     id gctx = ((id (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
183:         objc_getClass("NSGraphicsContext"),
184:         sel_registerName("currentContext"));
185:     if (!gctx) return nil;
186: 
187:     CGContextRef ctx = ((CGContextRef (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
188:         gctx, sel_registerName("CGContext"));
189:     if (!ctx) return nil;
190: 
191:     CGRect frame = CGRectMake(0, 0, CLOCK_WIDTH, CLOCK_HEIGHT);
192: 
193:     /*
194:      * Draw menu bar background matching WindowServer's menu bar colour.
195:      * WindowServer renders the menu bar as (0.96, 0.96, 0.96) — we match
196:      * that exactly so this window blends seamlessly into the menu bar.
197:      */
198:     CGContextSetRGBFillColor(ctx, 0.96, 0.96, 0.96, 1.0);
199:     CGContextFillRect(ctx, frame);
200: 
201:     /* Get current time string */
202:     char timebuf[8];
203:     _clockGetTimeString(timebuf, sizeof(timebuf));
204:     size_t timelen = 5;  /* "HH:MM" is always 5 chars */
205: 
206:     /*
207:      * Draw clock text — dark text on light menu bar, matching macOS.
208:      * macOS uses black text in the menu bar for the clock.
209:      * Each character is roughly 8px wide in the system bitmap font.
210:      */
211:     CGContextSetRGBFillColor(ctx, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0);
212: 
213:     CGFloat textWidth = (CGFloat)(timelen * 8);
214:     CGFloat textX = (CLOCK_WIDTH - textWidth) / 2.0;
215:     CGFloat textY = (CLOCK_HEIGHT - 12.0) / 2.0;  /* approx vertical centre */
216: 
217:     CGContextShowTextAtPoint(ctx, textX, textY, timebuf, timelen);
218: 
219:     return nil;
220: }
221: 
222: /* Global reference to clock window and view for periodic redraw */
223: static id g_clockWindow = nil;
224: static id g_clockView = nil;
225: 
226: /* ============================================================================
227:  * SystemUIServerDelegate — applicationDidFinishLaunching:
228:  * ============================================================================ */
229: 
230: static id _sysUIAppDidFinishLaunching(id self, SEL _cmd, id notification) {
231:     (void)self; (void)_cmd; (void)notification;
232: 
233:     /* --- Create clock window --- */
234:     id clockWindow;
235:     clockWindow = ((id (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
236:         (id)objc_getClass("NSWindow"),
237:         sel_registerName("alloc"));
238:     clockWindow = ((id (*)(id, SEL, CGRect, NSUInteger, int, BOOL))objc_msgSend)(
239:         clockWindow,
240:         sel_registerName("initWithContentRect:styleMask:backing:defer:"),
241:         CGRectMake(CLOCK_X, CLOCK_Y, CLOCK_WIDTH, CLOCK_HEIGHT),
242:         (NSUInteger)0,  /* NSWindowStyleMaskBorderless */
243:         (int)2,         /* NSBackingStoreBuffered */
244:         NO);
245: 
246:     ((void (*)(id, SEL, id))objc_msgSend)(
247:         clockWindow, sel_registerName("setTitle:"), (id)CFSTR("SystemUIServer"));
248: 
249:     /* Create ClockView class dynamically */
250:     Class ClockView = objc_allocateClassPair(objc_getClass("NSView"), "ClockView", 0);
251:     class_addMethod(ClockView, sel_registerName("drawRect:"),
252:                     (IMP)_ClockDrawRect, "v@:{CGRect=dddd}");
253:     objc_registerClassPair(ClockView);
254: 
255:     id clockView = ((id (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
256:         (id)ClockView, sel_registerName("alloc"));
257:     clockView = ((id (*)(id, SEL, CGRect))objc_msgSend)(
258:         clockView, sel_registerName("initWithFrame:"),
259:         CGRectMake(0, 0, CLOCK_WIDTH, CLOCK_HEIGHT));
260: 
261:     ((void (*)(id, SEL, id))objc_msgSend)(
262:         clockWindow, sel_registerName("setContentView:"), clockView);
263:     ((void (*)(id, SEL, id))objc_msgSend)(
264:         clockWindow, sel_registerName("makeKeyAndOrderFront:"), nil);
265: 
266:     /* Store global references for periodic clock updates */
267:     g_clockWindow = clockWindow;
268:     g_clockView = clockView;
269: 
270:     /* --- Set up menu --- */
271:     id menu = ((id (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
272:         (id)objc_getClass("NSMenu"), sel_registerName("alloc"));
273:     menu = ((id (*)(id, SEL, id))objc_msgSend)(
274:         menu, sel_registerName("initWithTitle:"), (id)CFSTR("SystemUIServer"));
275: 
276:     ((void (*)(id, SEL, id))objc_msgSend)(
277:         NSApp, sel_registerName("setMainMenu:"), menu);
278: 
279:     fprintf(stderr, "[SystemUIServer] Clock window created at (%d, %d) size %dx%d\n",
280:             CLOCK_X, CLOCK_Y, CLOCK_WIDTH, CLOCK_HEIGHT);
281:     return nil;
282: }
283: 
284: /* Track last minute drawn so we only redraw when the minute changes */
285: static int g_lastMinuteDrawn = -1;
286: 
287: /*
288:  * _clockNeedsUpdate — Check if the displayed minute has changed.
289:  *
290:  * Returns YES if the clock needs redrawing.
291:  */
292: static BOOL _clockNeedsUpdate(void) {
293:     CFAbsoluteTime now = CFAbsoluteTimeGetCurrent();
294:     if (now <= 0.0) return NO;
295: 
296:     double day_seconds = now - (double)((long)(now / 86400.0)) * 86400.0;
297:     if (day_seconds < 0.0) day_seconds += 86400.0;
298: 
299:     int total_minutes = (int)(day_seconds / 60.0);
300:     int current_minute = total_minutes % (24 * 60);
301: 
302:     if (current_minute != g_lastMinuteDrawn) {
303:         g_lastMinuteDrawn = current_minute;
304:         return YES;
305:     }
306:     return NO;
307: }
308: 
309: /* ============================================================================
310:  * main — Entry point
311:  *
312:  * Uses a custom run loop (like Terminal.app) to periodically update
313:  * the clock display.  On macOS, SystemUIServer uses NSTimer or
314:  * CFRunLoopTimer for this; we poll on each event loop iteration
315:  * since our AppKit doesn't have NSTimer support yet.
316:  * ============================================================================ */
317: 
318: int main(int argc, const char *argv[]) {
319:     (void)argc; (void)argv;
320:     void *pool = objc_autoreleasePoolPush();
321: 
322:     /* Create shared application */
323:     ((id (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
324:         (id)objc_getClass("NSApplication"),
325:         sel_registerName("sharedApplication"));
326: 
327:     /* Create delegate class dynamically */
328:     Class SystemUIServerDelegate = objc_allocateClassPair(
329:         objc_getClass("NSObject"), "SystemUIServerDelegate", 0);
330:     class_addMethod(SystemUIServerDelegate,
331:                     sel_registerName("applicationDidFinishLaunching:"),
332:                     (IMP)_sysUIAppDidFinishLaunching, "v@:@");
333:     objc_registerClassPair(SystemUIServerDelegate);
334: 
335:     /* Create delegate instance and set on NSApp */
336:     id delegate = ((id (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
337:         (id)SystemUIServerDelegate, sel_registerName("new"));
338:     ((void (*)(id, SEL, id))objc_msgSend)(
339:         NSApp, sel_registerName("setDelegate:"), delegate);
340: 
341:     /*
342:      * Custom run loop with clock update polling.
343:      *
344:      * On macOS, SystemUIServer uses NSTimer / CFRunLoopTimer to
345:      * schedule clock updates every 60 seconds.  Since our AppKit
346:      * doesn't have NSTimer yet, we check on each event loop
347:      * iteration whether the minute has changed.
348:      */
349:     ((void (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
350:         NSApp, sel_registerName("finishLaunching"));
351: 
352:     for (;;) {
353:         /* Poll WindowServer for events */
354:         id event = ((id (*)(id, SEL, NSUInteger, id, id, BOOL))objc_msgSend)(
355:             NSApp,
356:             sel_registerName("nextEventMatchingMask:untilDate:inMode:dequeue:"),
357:             (NSUInteger)0xFFFFFFFF,
358:             nil,
359:             (id)CFSTR("kCFRunLoopDefaultMode"),
360:             YES);
361: 
362:         if (event) {
363:             ((void (*)(id, SEL, id))objc_msgSend)(
364:                 NSApp, sel_registerName("sendEvent:"), event);
365:         }
366: 
367:         /* Check if clock needs updating (minute changed) */
368:         if (_clockNeedsUpdate() && g_clockView) {
369:             ((void (*)(id, SEL, BOOL))objc_msgSend)(
370:                 g_clockView, sel_registerName("setNeedsDisplay:"), YES);
371:         }
372: 
373:         /* Display dirty windows */
374:         if (g_clockWindow) {
375:             ((void (*)(id, SEL))objc_msgSend)(
376:                 g_clockWindow, sel_registerName("displayIfNeeded"));
377:         }
378:     }
379: 
380:     objc_autoreleasePoolPop(pool);
381:     return 0;
382: }
```

## Assistant (Build · claude-opus-4-6 · 15.4s)

Now I have a complete picture. This is a substantial task. Let me plan it carefully:

**What needs to happen for each app:**
1. Replace the freestanding preamble (inline typedefs + extern declarations) with `#import <AppKit/AppKit.h>`
2. Keep any app-specific extern declarations not covered by headers (POSIX syscalls, etc.)
3. Convert all `((ret_type (*)(id, SEL, ...))objc_msgSend)(target, sel_registerName("method"), ...)` patterns to `[target method:...]` syntax
4. Convert `objc_getClass("ClassName")` to `[ClassName ...]`
5. Keep runtime calls that are actually necessary: `objc_allocateClassPair`, `class_addMethod`, `objc_registerClassPair`, `objc_autoreleasePoolPush/Pop`
6. Update Makefiles to add `-I` paths

The key challenge: since these apps dynamically create classes at runtime with `objc_allocateClassPair`/`class_addMethod`, those parts MUST stay as C runtime calls. But all the message sends can become `[obj method]`.

Let me also check what ObjC runtime functions the headers provide vs what the apps need from the runtime directly:

**Tool: read**

**Input:**
```json
{
  "filePath": "/Users/viriya/dev/kiseki/userland/libobjc/objc"
}
```

No `limits.h`. That's going to be a problem if the apps try to include `<objc/runtime.h>`. But the apps need the runtime functions (`objc_allocateClassPair`, `class_addMethod`, `objc_registerClassPair`) which aren't declared in our framework headers. Let me think about the best approach.

For the ObjC conversion, the apps need:
1. `#import <AppKit/AppKit.h>` — gives them all types, classes, CG/CF/Foundation
2. Runtime functions for dynamic class creation — we can either:
   a. Include `<objc/runtime.h>` (but it needs `<limits.h>`)
   b. Just keep the specific `extern` declarations they need

Option (b) is simpler and avoids the `limits.h` issue. Let me also check if we need a `limits.h`:

```json
{
  "filePath": "/Users/viriya/dev/kiseki/userland/libsystem/include/limits.h",
  "content": "/*\n * Kiseki OS — <limits.h>\n *\n * Implementation limits for integral types (matching Darwin/arm64).\n */\n\n#ifndef _LIMITS_H_\n#define _LIMITS_H_\n\n/* char */\n#define CHAR_BIT    8\n#define SCHAR_MIN   (-128)\n#define SCHAR_MAX   127\n#define UCHAR_MAX   255\n#define CHAR_MIN    SCHAR_MIN\n#define CHAR_MAX    SCHAR_MAX\n\n/* short */\n#define SHRT_MIN    (-32768)\n#define SHRT_MAX    32767\n#define USHRT_MAX   65535\n\n/* int */\n#define INT_MIN     (-2147483647 - 1)\n#define INT_MAX     2147483647\n#define UINT_MAX    4294967295U\n\n/* long (64-bit on arm64) */\n#define LONG_MIN    (-9223372036854775807L - 1L)\n#define LONG_MAX    9223372036854775807L\n#define ULONG_MAX   18446744073709551615UL\n\n/* long long */\n#define LLONG_MIN   (-9223372036854775807LL - 1LL)\n#define LLONG_MAX   9223372036854775807LL\n#define ULLONG_MAX  18446744073709551615ULL\n\n/* POSIX required */\n#define SSIZE_MAX   LONG_MAX\n#define PATH_MAX    1024\n#define NAME_MAX    255\n\n/* MB_LEN_MAX */\n#define MB_LEN_MAX  6\n\n#endif /* _LIMITS_H_ */\n"
}
```

## Assistant (Build · claude-opus-4-6 · 21.0s)

Good, the LSP errors are just kernel files that can't find `kiseki/types.h` — those are pre-existing and not related to our work.

Now let me update all 4 Makefiles. The include paths need to be:
- `-I../../` — so `#import <AppKit/AppKit.h>` finds `userland/AppKit/AppKit.h`
- `-I../../libsystem/include` — so `#include <stdint.h>` finds our headers
- `-I../../libobjc` — so `#include <objc/runtime.h>` finds the runtime headers

Wait, actually the libobjc headers are at `userland/libobjc/objc/runtime.h`. So we need `-I../../libobjc` to resolve `#include <objc/runtime.h>` → `userland/libobjc/objc/runtime.h`. That's correct.

But wait — the `runtime.h` includes `<sys/types.h>`, `<limits.h>`, `<stdint.h>`, `<stddef.h>`. With `-I../../libsystem/include`, those would resolve to our freestanding headers. But the libobjc `runtime.h` also includes `"Availability.h"` and `"objc-visibility.h"` etc. which use relative includes from within the `objc/` directory. That should work since they're in the same directory.

Actually, let me re-examine this. The libobjc `runtime.h` includes:
- `"objc-visibility.h"` — relative, same dir
- `"objc-config.h"` — relative, same dir
- `<stdint.h>`, `<limits.h>`, `<stddef.h>`, `<sys/types.h>` — system headers
- `"Availability.h"` — relative, same dir

The system headers would be resolved via `-I../../libsystem/include`. But there's also `-isysroot $(SYSROOT)` which points to the macOS SDK. With `-nostdlib -ffreestanding`, the macOS SDK headers might still be found via isysroot. We need our headers to take priority.

The issue is that `-isysroot` sets the system header search root, and `-I` paths are searched before system paths. So `-I../../libsystem/include` should take priority for `<stdint.h>` etc. That's correct.

Let me also check: the current Makefiles use `-isysroot $(SYSROOT)` but with `-ffreestanding`. The `-isysroot` is needed so clang can find its internal headers. We should add our `-I` paths BEFORE the isysroot paths in the include search order. Since `-I` paths are searched before `-isysroot` paths, this should work.

Let me update all 4 Makefiles now:

```json
{
  "filePath": "/Users/viriya/dev/kiseki/userland/apps/Dock.app/Makefile",
  "oldString": "OBJCFLAGS := -target arm64-apple-macos11 \\\n             -fobjc-runtime=gnustep-1.9 \\\n             -isysroot $(SYSROOT) \\\n             -nostdlib -ffreestanding -fno-builtin \\\n             -fno-stack-protector \\\n             -mcpu=cortex-a53 \\\n             -O2 -Wall -Wextra \\\n             -Wno-unused-parameter \\\n             -Wno-unused-function \\\n             -Wno-objc-root-class \\\n             -Wno-deprecated-objc-isa-usage \\\n             -std=gnu11",

}
```

## Assistant (Build · claude-opus-4-6 · 21.5s)

Good — the libobjc headers are self-contained with simple includes. The `runtime.h` includes `<stdint.h>`, `<limits.h>`, `<stddef.h>`, `<sys/types.h>` — we have all of those now in `libsystem/include/`.

But wait, there's a potential issue. The libobjc `runtime.h` is 1228 lines and defines things like `id`, `Class`, `SEL`, `BOOL` etc. Our `Foundation.h` also defines `BOOL`, `nil`, `id` (via the `@interface NSObject` which uses `Class isa`). There could be typedef conflicts.

## Assistant (Build · claude-opus-4-6 · 64.7s)

There's a conflict: the runtime.h defines `BOOL` as `unsigned char`, but our Foundation.h defines it as `_Bool`. Also, `id` is defined differently. The runtime.h defines `id` as `struct objc_object *`, while our Foundation.h also defines it implicitly via `@interface NSObject { Class isa; }`.

Given these conflicts, the cleanest approach is to NOT include `<objc/runtime.h>` at all, and instead keep a small block of `extern` declarations for the few runtime functions the apps need. This avoids all the typedef conflicts and is simpler.

The runtime functions the apps need are:
- `objc_allocateClassPair`
- `class_addMethod`
- `objc_registerClassPair`
- `objc_autoreleasePoolPush` / `objc_autoreleasePoolPop`

These are already available via the ObjC compiler's implicit knowledge of the runtime, but for the C runtime API calls we need explicit externs.

Now let me start rewriting the apps. I'll start with Dock.m since it's the simplest (366 lines), then SystemUIServer (382 lines), then Finder (1096 lines), then Terminal (1318 lines).

Let me write the new Dock.m:

**Tool: write**

**Input:**
```json
{
  "filePath": "/Users/viriya/dev/kiseki/userland/apps/Dock.app/Dock.m",
  "content": "/*\n * Kiseki OS - Dock.app\n *\n * The Dock process, matching macOS architecture:\n *   - Renders the dock bar along the bottom of the screen\n *   - Renders the desktop wallpaper (solid colour for now)\n *   - Runs as a separate AppKit process launched by loginwindow\n *\n * On macOS, the Dock renders the desktop background (via\n * CGSSetDesktopBackground/CGSSetDesktopImage) and the dock bar.\n * Our Dock creates two windows:\n *   1. Desktop window — fullscreen, sent to back, fills with wallpaper\n *   2. Dock bar window — bottom strip, always on top\n *\n * The dock bar draws a translucent grey strip with application icons\n * (represented as simple coloured squares for now).\n *\n * Compiled with COMDAT-stripping pipeline (same as AppKit).\n */\n\n#import <AppKit/AppKit.h>\n\n/* ============================================================================\n * ObjC runtime functions for dynamic class creation\n *\n * These are not declared in our framework headers because the apps'\n * framework headers are for consumer use, not runtime internals.\n * ============================================================================ */\n\nextern Class  objc_allocateClassPair(Class superclass, const char *name, size_t extraBytes);\nextern void   objc_registerClassPair(Class cls);\nextern BOOL   class_addMethod(Class cls, SEL name, IMP imp, const char *types);\nextern void  *objc_autoreleasePoolPush(void);\nextern void   objc_autoreleasePoolPop(void *pool);\n\n/* C library extras not in framework headers */\nextern int    fprintf(void *stream, const char *fmt, ...);\nextern void **__stderrp;\n#define stderr (*__stderrp)\n\n/* Screen dimensions (matching WindowServer) */\n#define SCREEN_WIDTH    1280\n#define SCREEN_HEIGHT   800\n#define DOCK_HEIGHT     54\n#define DOCK_ICON_SIZE  40\n#define DOCK_ICON_PAD   8\n#define DOCK_Y          (SCREEN_HEIGHT - DOCK_HEIGHT)\n\n/* ============================================================================\n * DockView — Custom NSView subclass for the dock bar\n * ============================================================================ */\n\n/* We create DockView dynamically using the ObjC runtime since we can't\n * use @interface/@implementation without the full class hierarchy being\n * in this compilation unit. Instead, we use the C runtime API. */\n\n/* Dock icon entries */\ntypedef struct {\n    const char *label;\n    CGFloat r, g, b;    /* Icon colour */\n} DockIcon;\n\nstatic DockIcon dock_icons[] = {\n    { \"Finder\",   0.25, 0.55, 0.95 },  /* Blue */\n    { \"Terminal\",  0.15, 0.15, 0.15 },  /* Dark grey */\n    { \"System\",   0.70, 0.70, 0.70 },  /* Light grey */\n};\nstatic int dock_icon_count = 3;\n\n/*\n * _DockDrawRect — drawRect: implementation for DockView\n */\nstatic id _DockDrawRect(id self, SEL _cmd, CGRect dirtyRect) {\n    (void)_cmd; (void)dirtyRect;\n\n    /* Get the backing CGContext from NSGraphicsContext */\n    NSGraphicsContext *gctx = [NSGraphicsContext currentContext];\n    if (!gctx) return nil;\n\n    CGContextRef ctx = [gctx CGContext];\n    if (!ctx) return nil;\n\n    /* For the dock view, frame is 0,0 to SCREEN_WIDTH x DOCK_HEIGHT */\n    CGRect frame = CGRectMake(0, 0, SCREEN_WIDTH, DOCK_HEIGHT);\n\n    /* Draw translucent dark background */\n    CGContextSetRGBFillColor(ctx, 0.15, 0.15, 0.15, 0.85);\n    CGContextFillRect(ctx, frame);\n\n    /* Draw a subtle top separator line */\n    CGContextSetRGBFillColor(ctx, 0.3, 0.3, 0.3, 1.0);\n    CGContextFillRect(ctx, CGRectMake(0, 0, SCREEN_WIDTH, 1));\n\n    /* Draw dock icons centred */\n    CGFloat totalWidth = (CGFloat)dock_icon_count * (DOCK_ICON_SIZE + DOCK_ICON_PAD) - DOCK_ICON_PAD;\n    CGFloat startX = (SCREEN_WIDTH - totalWidth) / 2.0;\n    CGFloat iconY = (DOCK_HEIGHT - DOCK_ICON_SIZE) / 2.0;\n\n    for (int i = 0; i < dock_icon_count; i++) {\n        CGFloat ix = startX + (CGFloat)i * (DOCK_ICON_SIZE + DOCK_ICON_PAD);\n        CGRect iconRect = CGRectMake(ix, iconY, DOCK_ICON_SIZE, DOCK_ICON_SIZE);\n\n        /* Icon background (rounded rectangle approximated as filled rect) */\n        CGContextSetRGBFillColor(ctx, dock_icons[i].r, dock_icons[i].g,\n                                  dock_icons[i].b, 1.0);\n        CGContextFillRect(ctx, iconRect);\n\n        /* Icon border */\n        CGContextSetRGBStrokeColor(ctx, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 0.3);\n        CGContextSetLineWidth(ctx, 1.0);\n        CGContextStrokeRect(ctx, iconRect);\n\n        /* Label below icon (first char) — white text */\n        CGContextSetRGBFillColor(ctx, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 0.9);\n        const char *label = dock_icons[i].label;\n        size_t llen = strlen(label);\n        CGFloat textW = (CGFloat)(llen * 8);\n        CGFloat textX = ix + (DOCK_ICON_SIZE - textW) / 2.0;\n        if (textX < ix) textX = ix;\n        /* Draw text inside the icon square, vertically centred */\n        CGFloat textY = iconY + (DOCK_ICON_SIZE - 16.0) / 2.0;\n        CGContextShowTextAtPoint(ctx, textX, textY, label, llen > 5 ? 5 : llen);\n    }\n\n    return nil;\n}\n\n/* ============================================================================\n * DesktopView — Custom NSView for the desktop wallpaper\n * ============================================================================ */\n\nstatic id _DesktopDrawRect(id self, SEL _cmd, CGRect dirtyRect) {\n    (void)_cmd; (void)dirtyRect;\n\n    NSGraphicsContext *gctx = [NSGraphicsContext currentContext];\n    if (!gctx) return nil;\n\n    CGContextRef ctx = [gctx CGContext];\n    if (!ctx) return nil;\n\n    /* Draw a macOS-style gradient wallpaper (simplified: solid teal-blue) */\n    CGContextSetRGBFillColor(ctx, 0.05, 0.25, 0.45, 1.0);\n    CGContextFillRect(ctx, CGRectMake(0, 0, SCREEN_WIDTH, SCREEN_HEIGHT));\n\n    /* Draw a subtle gradient effect using horizontal bands */\n    for (int y = 0; y < SCREEN_HEIGHT; y += 4) {\n        CGFloat t = (CGFloat)y / (CGFloat)SCREEN_HEIGHT;\n        CGFloat r = 0.05 + t * 0.10;\n        CGFloat g = 0.20 + t * 0.15;\n        CGFloat b = 0.40 + t * 0.15;\n        CGContextSetRGBFillColor(ctx, r, g, b, 1.0);\n        CGContextFillRect(ctx, CGRectMake(0, y, SCREEN_WIDTH, 4));\n    }\n\n    return nil;\n}\n\n/* ============================================================================\n * DockAppDelegate — applicationDidFinishLaunching:\n * ============================================================================ */\n\nstatic id _dockAppDidFinishLaunching(id self, SEL _cmd, id notification) {\n    (void)_cmd; (void)notification;\n\n    /* --- Create desktop background window --- */\n    NSWindow *desktopWindow = [[NSWindow alloc]\n        initWithContentRect:CGRectMake(0, 0, SCREEN_WIDTH, SCREEN_HEIGHT)\n                  styleMask:NSWindowStyleMaskBorderless\n                    backing:NSBackingStoreBuffered\n                      defer:NO];\n\n    [desktopWindow setTitle:(id)CFSTR(\"Desktop\")];\n\n    /* Create DesktopView class dynamically */\n    Class DesktopView = objc_allocateClassPair([NSView class], \"DesktopView\", 0);\n    class_addMethod(DesktopView, @selector(drawRect:),\n                    (IMP)_DesktopDrawRect, \"v@:{CGRect=dddd}\");\n    objc_registerClassPair(DesktopView);\n\n    NSView *desktopView = [[DesktopView alloc]\n        initWithFrame:CGRectMake(0, 0, SCREEN_WIDTH, SCREEN_HEIGHT)];\n\n    [desktopWindow setContentView:desktopView];\n\n    /*\n     * Order desktop to back — behind all other windows.\n     * On macOS, the desktop window uses NSWindowLevelDesktop (kCGDesktopWindowLevel)\n     * and orderBack:.  Our WindowServer supports WS_ORDER_BACK.\n     */\n    [desktopWindow orderBack:nil];\n\n    /* --- Create dock bar window --- */\n    NSWindow *dockWindow = [[NSWindow alloc]\n        initWithContentRect:CGRectMake(0, DOCK_Y, SCREEN_WIDTH, DOCK_HEIGHT)\n                  styleMask:NSWindowStyleMaskBorderless\n                    backing:NSBackingStoreBuffered\n                      defer:NO];\n\n    [dockWindow setTitle:(id)CFSTR(\"Dock\")];\n\n    /* Create DockView class dynamically */\n    Class DockView = objc_allocateClassPair([NSView class], \"DockView\", 0);\n    class_addMethod(DockView, @selector(drawRect:),\n                    (IMP)_DockDrawRect, \"v@:{CGRect=dddd}\");\n    objc_registerClassPair(DockView);\n\n    NSView *dockView = [[DockView alloc]\n        initWithFrame:CGRectMake(0, 0, SCREEN_WIDTH, DOCK_HEIGHT)];\n\n    [dockWindow setContentView:dockView];\n    [dockWindow makeKeyAndOrderFront:nil];\n\n    /* --- Set up menu --- */\n    NSMenu *menu = [[NSMenu alloc] initWithTitle:(id)CFSTR(\"Dock\")];\n    [NSApp setMainMenu:menu];\n\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[Dock] Desktop and dock bar windows created\\n\");\n    return nil;\n}\n\n/* ============================================================================\n * main — Entry point\n * ============================================================================ */\n\nint main(int argc, const char *argv[]) {\n    (void)argc; (void)argv;\n    void *pool = objc_autoreleasePoolPush();\n\n    /* Create shared application */\n    [NSApplication sharedApplication];\n\n    /* Create delegate class dynamically */\n    Class DockAppDelegate = objc_allocateClassPair(\n        [NSObject class], \"DockAppDelegate\", 0);\n    class_addMethod(DockAppDelegate,\n                    @selector(applicationDidFinishLaunching:),\n                    (IMP)_dockAppDidFinishLaunching, \"v@:@\");\n    objc_registerClassPair(DockAppDelegate);\n\n    /* Create delegate instance and set on NSApp */\n    id delegate = [DockAppDelegate new];\n    [NSApp setDelegate:delegate];\n\n    /* Run the event loop */\n    [NSApp run];\n\n    objc_autoreleasePoolPop(pool);\n    return 0;\n}\n"
}
```
